title: crataegus × subsphaerica subsp. domicensis
intro: Crataegus × subsphaerica subsp. domicensis, also known as the hybrid hawthorn, is a plant species that combines the characteristics of Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus laevigata. This plant is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular benefits. The health benefits of Crataegus × subsphaerica subsp. domicensis include reducing blood pressure, lowering cholesterol levels, and improving heart health. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
actions: Crataegus × subsphaerica subsp. domicensis has many therapeutic actions, such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. Its analgesic and antispasmodic properties can provide relief from pain and muscle spasms, improving sleep quality and reducing stress levels, ultimately enhancing overall well-being and daily functioning. Additionally, the plant's hepatoprotective and cardioprotective properties can help protect against liver and heart diseases, allowing individuals to engage in physical activities and maintain a healthy lifestyle. By harnessing the therapeutic actions of Crataegus × subsphaerica subsp. domicensis, people can experience improved cardiovascular health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
constituents: Crataegus × subsphaerica subsp. domicensis has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, triterpenoids including ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, and phenolic acids like ellagic acid and gallic acid. These compounds have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can improve daily life by reducing the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and diabetes, as well as providing relief from symptoms associated with conditions like arthritis and allergies. The flavonoids present in Crataegus × subsphaerica subsp. domicensis also have cardiovascular protective effects, which can help lower blood pressure and improve circulation, while the phenolic acids have been shown to have antimicrobial and antitumor activities. Additionally, the triterpenoids in this plant have been found to have hepatoprotective effects, which can help protect the liver from damage and promote overall health and well-being.
